description: CTI Law: Biological V1 neurons (DANDI:000039, two-photon calcium imaging)
modality: Two-photon calcium imaging, mouse visual cortex V1
stimulus: Drifting gratings (contrast tuning), 8 directions x 6 contrasts
reason: Insufficient data: session DFF recording covers only 36 min (2183s) of 18-hour epoch table; only 22/720 epochs within DFF range; N=15 neurons too small; kappa range [0.085,0.37] with near-chance accuracy for all classes
root_cause: DANDI:000039 is a CONTRAST TUNING dataset, not orientation tuning. Neurons respond to contrast changes, not direction changes. The specific session has only 22 usable imaging epochs (DFF recording covers first 36 min only). Each epoch is 60s of one direction+contrast. With N=15 neurons, representations are noisy and near-chance separable.
future_work: Requires (1) sessions with full DFF-epoch overlap, (2) N>=50 neurons, (3) proper orientation tuning protocol (not contrast tuning), OR allensdk access to Allen Brain Observatory Visual Coding dataset
